title: Explainer-How will Marine Le Pen's legal problems affect her presidential campaign?

fullArticle:
Sign up now: Get ST's newsletters delivered to your inbox

The Paris appeals court found Marine Le Pen guilty of misusing EU funds to pay party staff and sentenced her to three years in prison.

PARIS – French far-right leader Marine Le Pen is running for president, but unresolved legal questions loom over her campaign.

Below are attempts to address the main issues:

Le Pen’s embezzlement conviction was upheld on July 7, but she said she would appeal to France’s highest court, the Cour de Cassation.

The Paris appeals court found Le Pen guilty of misusing European Union funds to pay party staff and sentenced her to three years in prison, with two suspended and one to be served in home detention with an electronic tag.
